description_long: This is a single-story residential house with a gable roof covered in reddish-brown tiles. The front facade features a small porch area with a stone-tiled floor, a light-colored main door, and two sets of rectangular windows, one of which is shaded by a prominent orange awning. The exterior walls are finished in a smooth, light cream color, with a section of brickwork visible on the lower right side of the front. The side and rear elevations are plain, with no visible windows or doors, emphasizing the simplicity of the structure. The roof extends symmetrically along the length of the house, and the overall design is compact and functional, suitable for suburban or urban neighborhoods.
description: A compact, single-story house with a gable roof, light-colored walls, a small porch, and a mix of stone, brick, and tile finishes.
description_view_0: Front view showing the porch, main door, two windows, stone-tiled entry, and brickwork detail.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the long, plain wall and the full length of the tiled gable roof.
description_view_2: Rear view highlighting the simple, unadorned wall and the symmetrical gable roof.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with a plain wall and partial visibility of the porch and roof overhang.
attribute_entryway_design: Simple entry with a small stone-tiled porch, single door, and an orange awning above one window.
